headline: Hezbollah Drone Strike Kills Israeli Commander, Sparks Massive Israeli Air Campaign
subheadline: Israel launched over 150 air strikes in Lebanon since midnight, hitting more than 80 Hezbollah targets after the drone‑borne attack on an IDF tank.
lede: Hezbollah used a drone to hit an IDF tank of the 52nd Battalion in Tabnin, killing battalion commander Dor Ben‑Shamchon and three soldiers. Israel responded with more than 150 air strikes on Lebanon, including over 80 specific Hezbollah positions. Lebanese authorities report 47 civilian deaths and 97 injuries from the Israeli bombardment. Diplomatic pressure from Iran, the United States and France seeks to curb the escalation, but negotiations remain uncertain.

sections.title: Ukrainian Front Faces Drone and Artillery Strikes
sections.body: Two Ukrainian UAVs were downed near the north side of Sevastopol. Russian drones struck a logistics operator building in Zaporizhzhia, injuring eight and causing a fire. In Kherson, Russian forces damaged two public‑transport buses, wounding five, while strikes on Kramatorsk killed two and injured at least six. Russian troops reported destroying a surrounded enemy in Konstantinovka and advancing in the Nikolaevsky microdistrict.

sections.title: Hezbollah Drone Attack Triggers Massive Israeli Strikes
sections.body: Hezbollah’s drone hit an IDF tank of the 52nd Battalion in Tabnin, killing commander Dor Ben‑Shamchon and three soldiers. The Israeli Defence Forces reported striking more than 80 Hezbollah targets and carrying out over 150 air strikes in Lebanon since midnight. Lebanese Health Ministry figures record 47 civilian deaths and 97 injuries from the Israeli response. Iran demanded guarantees for ending the war in Lebanon, while France urged Israel to halt strikes and called on the US to apply pressure.

sections.title: Intelligence Assessment
sections.body: Hezbollah’s successful drone‑borne attack on armored units shows a growing precision capability that threatens Israeli ground operations. Israel’s willingness to conduct extensive air campaigns despite civilian casualties raises the risk of broader regional escalation, especially as Iranian diplomatic leverage intensifies.
